data.title: ETH leads as money supply hits record high; BTC dips, US crypto inflows reach $60b YTD, ETH could outperform BTC for 6 months.
data.description: Bitcoin experiences a decrease as the recent job data results lower expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut. The US witnesses a significant surge in cryptocurrency inflows, reaching $60 billion year-to-date. Strategy firm decides to increase preferred stock offering to $2 billion. Tokyo's Quantum Solutions expresses plans to acquire 3,000 bitcoins. Analyst Novogratz suggests that Ethereum may outperform Bitcoin for the next six months. Galaxy initiates the sale of large quantities of bitcoins. BitMine announces intentions for a $2.5 billion stock sale to purchase Ethereum. SOL founder maps out plans for low-latency on-chain trading. WindTree Therapeutics secures $520 million for buying Binance Coin. Bank of America anticipates a $75 billion increase in stablecoin supply. Celestia Foundation acquires $62.5 million worth of TIA. TON Foundation partners with Kingsway to establish a treasury company. Tron Inc. makes entrance into Nasdaq. Christie's introduces a $1 billion real estate division for cryptocurrency transactions. Zircuit launches an AI-driven trading engine. Woo X platform gets hacked for $12 million.

data.title: Skyward Specialty reschedules second quarter earnings call to July 31 at 12 PM EDT.
data.description: Skyward Specialty Insurance Group, Inc.™ (NASDAQ: SKWD) based in Houston, revealed a rescheduling of its second quarter earnings call. The call, initially set for a different date, will now take place on Thursday, July 31 at 12:00 p.m. EDT.

data.title: The Alliance for Aging Research warns against weakening IP protections to improve patient access to lifesaving medicines.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, a report from the Alliance for Aging Research highlighted concerns regarding policy proposals that could undermine intellectual property rights. At a recent gathering of national experts, individual presentations underscored the importance of this issue. The Alliance emphasized the potential negative impact of such proposals, urging policymakers to consider the implications. The report follows discussions at the event earlier this year, where key figures expressed worries about the potential consequences of weakening intellectual property protections on innovation. The Alliance's stance is supported by data that show the significant economic value and job creation associated with strong intellectual property rights. They caution that any changes in this area could hinder progress and innovation in various sectors.

data.title: St. James Gold Corp. (TSX-V: LORD) reinstates trading on TSX Venture Exchange on July 29, 2025.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on July 25, 2025, St. James Gold Corp. shared that their common shares will resume trading on the TSX Venture Exchange around July 29, 2025. The company, identified as St. James and listed as TSXV: LORD, OTCQB: LRDJF, FSE: BVU3, revealed that the Exchange has finalized its evaluation for this reinstatement.

data.title: Inventor from Baker, LA creates new mirror, 'The Styling Companion', to simplify hairstyling tasks.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on July 25, 2025, an inventor from Baker, Louisiana, shared the creation of a specialized mirror aimed at streamlining hairstyling processes. Known as THE STYLING COMPANION, this innovative design addresses the challenge of simultaneously holding a mirror and styling hair. The inventor expressed the motive behind this creation, stating a desire to simplify tasks. This mirror offers a solution to this common issue, catering to individuals looking for efficiency in hairstyling.

data.title: Proteomics market set to grow at 13% CAGR till 2030, driven by advanced technologies for precision medicine.
data.description: The growth of precision medicine is being fueled by developments in proteomics technologies, leading to increased interest in early diagnostics, personalized treatments, and a better understanding of disease mechanisms. A recent study from BCC Research, released on July 25, 2025 in Boston, highlights this trend.

data.title: Gilead's new HIV prevention drug Yeztugo approved by FDA, with ≥99.9% efficacy in trials. European approval pending.
data.description: The U.S. FDA has approved Gilead Sciences' Yeztugo (lenacapavir) as a new PrEP option to reduce the risk of sexually acquired HIV in individuals weighing at least 35kg. This is the first and only twice-yearly PrEP option in the U.S. Data from the Phase 3 trials showed that >99.9% of participants who took Yeztugo remained HIV negative. Gilead's first PrEP medication was approved in the U.S. in 2012. In Europe, the CHMP of the EMA has given a positive opinion for lenacapavir as PrEP. The final EC decision is expected later this year. Gilead has committed to providing no-profit access for 2 million people globally. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: David Ellison faces major challenges at Paramount: cable business down, news division in turmoil, and A.I. threat to movies.
data.description: The cable industry has significantly declined, with a notable decrease in business. The news division is currently facing upheaval and uncertainty. Additionally, the film industry is gearing up for the impact of artificial intelligence technology. These challenges are only the tip of the iceberg.

data.title: Soybean Oil Market projected to hit $229.06 billion by 2033 from $89.91 billion in 2024, with 10.95% CAGR till 2033.
data.description: In Dublin, a recent report on the Soybean Oil Market forecasts significant growth, with the market expected to reach a value of US$229.06 billion by 2033. This marks a substantial increase from US$89.91 billion recorded in 2024, reflecting an impressive Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 10.95% from 2025 to 2033. The surge in demand for plant-based oils, rising health awareness among consumers, the versatile applications in both food and industrial sectors, the increasing utilization for biodiesel production, are highlighted as key factors driving this growth trajectory.

data.title: Meta to end political ads in E.U. in October due to new E.U. regulation, claiming it has "unworkable requirements."
data.description: Meta announced that political advertising would cease in October due to a new E.U. regulation with challenging requirements ahead.
